benefits: Telis crassipes, also known as the giant water lily, has been found to have various health benefits. It is used in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots are said to have antioxidant and antibacterial properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. The leaves of the giant water lily are also used to make tea, which is believed to aid in digestion and reduce stress. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its health benefits and potential uses.
